raw_consumer_statement:
This complaint concerns the improper denial of two billing disputes against a merchant who failed to provide the agreed-upon services. 

On XX/XX/year>, I made an initial payment of {$1500.00}, and a final payment of {$1500.00} was made on XX/XX/year>, for a total of {$3000.00}. This was for a complete, functional website from the merchant XXXX XXXX XXXX. The merchant failed to deliver a functional product, providing a website with critical, unresolved technical issues, such as unacceptable loading speeds and a non-functional shopping cart. Due to this failure to provide the contracted service, I formally canceled the service on XX/XX/year>. 

I opened disputes for both transactions and provided the bank with extensive documentation to support my claim. This evidence included the signed service agreement, a chronological summary of my communications with the merchant from early XXXX detailing the unresolved issues, and objective third-party performance reports. I also provided a letter explaining how the merchant 's actions violated the governing Florida Deceptive and Unfair Trade Practices Act. 

Despite this clear and overwhelming evidence, on XX/XX/year>, I received a letter stating the investigation was complete and that the " documentation or facts provided don't support the billing error/dispute ''. I believe the bank failed to properly review the evidence and has unfairly held me responsible for charges where the service was never rendered as promised in the contract.
